svg.title: TradersApp - Architecture Roadmap
svg.desc: Roadmap view that separates current state, stabilization work, extraction work, and eventual operating targets across the major architecture lanes.
svg.text: Architecture roadmap
This chart is intentionally a planning view. Only the first column describes present reality.
Lane
Today
Stabilize
Extract
Operate
Request path
CURRENT
BFF -> analysis-service -> HTTP /predict behind the seam.
Unify SLA language around less than 200ms P95 and verify fallback policy.
Move more analysis behavior into analysis-service without breaking the contract.
Optional strict gRPC-only serving once the seam is independently hardened.
Bounded contexts
CURRENT
Analysis extracted first. Ingestion and learning remain logical boundaries.
Keep manifest, docs, and proto comments aligned before adding more deployables.
Pull ingestion and learning out in slices once ownership and contracts are stable.
One service per bounded context with clearer team ownership and lower coupling.
Learning + data
PARTIAL
Airflow, GE, MLflow, Kafka, MinIO, and PostgreSQL exist and support the loop.
Prove the live PostgreSQL, lag, and data-quality path in target environments.
Shift retraining and feedback execution behind a cleaner learning boundary.
Closed-loop promotion, rollback, and lineage with explicit approval points.
Controls + observability
PARTIAL
Observability and policy systems exist, but maturity is uneven across environments.
Verify alerts, traces, secret sync, and policy enforcement in a real cluster flow.
Attach ownership and status labels to each control-plane component.
Controls become dependable operating guardrails instead of slideware.
Delivery story
MIXED
Public-cloud deploy path is current in docs. Self-hosted CI/CD and k3s are partial.
Pick the primary path per environment and stop mixing current and target language.
Separate deployment docs into current, partial, and target views.
One validated release path per environment, with rollback and verification rehearsed.
